item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Both of the images used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC-BY-SA 3.0) as both images have NC and ND in their licenses. Thus, the images have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the section about B13 (wolves) it talks about packs of B16 (frogs) which seemed odd. I am guessing its an error but I wanted to double check.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh. Oh dear. I assume that "alizarin" is intended to be "the Scarlet One" rather than the "the squeezings," which is what the literal Arabic implies?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…including the observation post personnel just happening to have the same names as the members of Justice.

Motherfucker how did I not notice this earlier

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're the first person in 4 years to bring up my way of naming personnel, lol. I can change the Justice's names, but Perhacs is going to have to remain unchanged for story purposes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the article, but it kinda ruined my immersion for most of the named characters to have the exact names of famous musicians, including the observation post personnel just happening to have the same names as the members of Justice.

Although I enjoyed the references, I'd prefer if they were a little more oblique.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You very nearly made me throw up. Since my gag reflex is nearly dead, that's a high compliment.

Take my upvote and never make me read about a sea of literal saliva and mucus again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Entirely cringe-inducing, horrific, and disgusting. Extremely good use of biological grossness. Everyone else got all the things that I'd have pointed out. Otherwise, good job! I don't get grossed out easily but you've gone and done it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hadn't thought of it like that. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also, why did you specifically say that they're evolving -As instead of just human mouths?

I refer to them as both, because they all have the same genetic origin. They all belong to the same person, so the Foundation assumed they stemmed from the largest instance of them. Fixed the drone sentence as well.

Can't argue with the your dislike of the details, since I wanted to highlight the adaptability of -B species, while easing the reader out of the initial grossness of "this is a sea of spit and mucus chunks". I'll see if I can trim a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Editing watermarks out is a definite faux pas. Those get added for a reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Was the watermark on the deer photo intentional? It wouldn't be hard to edit out.

Also, why did you specifically say that they're evolving -As instead of just human mouths?

Your description of the drone has some grammar issues.

All in all.. I'm definitely fascinated to see where this goes, but I don't think the weird twist ending satisfied the amount of disgusting random detail I had to read through. I understand that was the point, but a lot of the animal descriptions didn't seem to add much of interest or particularly make sense.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have made this fact more clear. 👏

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Second redaction was just a team that wasn't an MTF. Edited to include only one redaction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Making a new family-based GoI?

Er, unless I'm missing something, directly from the author post:

Also noting: THIS IS NOT A GOI, THIS IS A SET OF SEQUENTIAL ARTICLES alright

Edit: Holy shit it's over 9000 now….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this, but I didn't like the overredaction for the wolves.

Warnings of [REDACTED]. Neutralization of all instances of B13 is currently the top priority of [REDACTED].

It felt unnecessary to even have the entry if you're going to redact everything of interest. Maybe if one or the other was redacted, and the rest was shown to give some idea of what might have been covered up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Making a new family-based GoI? I'm a little concerned it can't stand on its own; that's the only thing keeping me from an upvote, because this is otherwise pleasantly weird.

item.title: I understand everything
item.content:encoded: I finally understand the perhacs reference in your 4th part of this :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An estimated 22km3 liters of human saliva and phlegm has currently flooded SCP-2825. With the current amount of saliva produced, the saliva "lake" possesses a peak depth of 189m

This legitimately made me gag, as did most of the rest of the article. This is probably the grossest thing since the fly-filled cat1, and that's really saying something.

I'm really interested to see where this series' narrative goes, and it's been explored well thus far.

Footnotes
1. I can't remember the item number, but it's a weird misshapen cat and you can hear flies buzzing inside its body as it decomposes. Edit: Found it. SCP-511.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Jacob Perhacs, Part 2.

Accompaniment while reading: Mark Pritchard - Beautiful People (Feat. Thom Yorke)

Image Sources: Deer from WBUR, Boston's NPR News Station, Valley by Mallix.

Thanks to the Roget man, A Random Day, and tretter for feedback.

Also noting:

THIS IS NOT A GOI, THIS IS A SET OF SEQUENTIAL ARTICLES alright

Follow up with this article: SCP-2745
